assertions.expectation: Identifies PDB as the most likely/common cause of stuck upgrades
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Check for PDB blocking drain (most common cause)' and later mentions 'Start with checking PDBs first - that's the cause in ~80% of stuck drain scenarios'

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl command to check PDBs (kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces or similar)
assertions.reason: Provides 'kubectl get pdb -A -o wide' command to check PDBs across all namespaces

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify which pods are blocking the drain
assertions.reason: Includes multiple commands: 'kubectl get pods -A | grep Terminating', 'kubectl get pods -A | grep Pending', and event checking commands to identify blocking pods

assertions.expectation: Includes resource constraint diagnosis (capacity issues, pending pods)
assertions.reason: Includes commands to check for pending pods and scheduling failures, plus mentions increasing surge capacity for resource-constrained scenarios

assertions.expectation: Provides a fix for restrictive PDBs (patch or temporary adjustment)
assertions.reason: Provides explicit kubectl patch command to temporarily relax PDBs by setting maxUnavailable to 100%

assertions.expectation: Includes guidance on surge upgrade settings as a potential fix
assertions.reason: Provides gcloud command to increase max-surge-upgrade and set max-unavailable-upgrade as Fix 3

assertions.expectation: Mentions webhook issues as a possible cause
assertions.reason: Response does not mention admission webhooks or webhook-related issues as a potential cause of stuck drains

assertions.expectation: Includes validation steps to confirm the fix is working
assertions.reason: Includes a dedicated 'Validation' section with commands to watch upgrade progress, check for stuck pods, and monitor operations
